text: In a typical Git workflow, developers use feature branches to work on new changes without affecting the main codebase. Each feature branch is created from the main branch, allowing isolated development. Once the work is complete, the developer creates a pull request to propose the changes. Team members review the code and discuss improvements before it is merged. Merging integrates the feature branch into the main branch, creating a new version. This version control workflow helps teams collaborate efficiently, reduces conflicts, and maintains a clear history of changes. Understanding branching is essential for managing both small and large projects.
